chapter_title.en: Prayer of the Sick, the Unconcious, the Weak and the Infirm, the Elderly and Others

text: 1061 - وَ سَأَلَ عَبْدُ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ اَلْحَجَّاجِ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: عَنِ اَلْغَمْزِ يُصِيبُ اَلرَّجُلَ فِي بَطْنِهِ وَ هُوَ يَسْتَطِيعُ أَنْ يَصْبِرَ عَلَيْهِ أَ يُصَلِّي عَلَى تِلْكَ اَلْحَالَةِ أَمْ لاَ يُصَلِّي فَقَالَ «إِنِ اِحْتَمَلَ اَلصَّبْرَ وَ لَمْ يَخَفْ إِعْجَالاً عَنِ اَلصَّلاَةِ فَلْيُصَلِّ وَ لْيَصْبِرْ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.1061 - Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj asked Abu al-Hasan (as) about a man who feels discomfort in his stomach but is able to bear it. He asked whether the man should continue praying in that situation or not.
Imam (as) replied: "If he can tolerate the pain and does not fear it causing him to rush the prayer, then he should pray and be patient."
